evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-auth/plan.md. Assume checklists/ exists with ux.md (all complete) and test.md (3 incomplete items). Produce the exact user-facing response for the checklist gate step and what happens next.

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-notify/plan.md. Assume all checklists pass. evals.expected_output: The run provides controller-owned wave planning, launches separate parallel implementation subagents for the independent [P] test tasks, keeps sequential and TDD-dependent work out of that wave, and defines checkpoint validation requirements before advancing.
expectations: Includes all five progress reporting sections: Checklist Gate, Implementation Context Loaded, Phase Execution, Code Review Findings, Completion Validation
States that the controller owns the wave plan rather than delegating phase-wide scheduling to one subagent
Launches separate implementation subagents in parallel for T003, T004, and T005 (or equivalent explicit same-wave dispatch)
Keeps T006 sequential and keeps T007 after T003 because TDD or dependency ordering still applies
Defines a checkpoint decision format or equivalent explicit pass/fail criteria before advancing
References TDD-first ordering where test tasks run before their corresponding implementation tasks

Identifies mismatch between reviewer-reported lists and authoritative scope
Reports missing and extra files based on reconciliation

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-auth/plan.md. All checklists pass. You are in the Tests phase executing task T003: 'Write unit tests for JWT token validation in tests/auth/jwt.test.ts' (this is a TDD RED step — the implementation doesn't exist yet). You wrote 4 test cases. When you run `npm test -- tests/auth/jwt.test.ts`, the tests execute and all 4 fail with 'Error: Cannot find module src/auth/jwt'. Distinguishes between a clean RED failure (tests fail for the intended reason) and a broken test (syntax errors, import crashes)
References TDD-first or RED-GREEN workflow as the reason this counts as success
Does NOT treat the test failures as an error that blocks progress
